title: cert-3 stabilization: home-link → regression was already fixed in tree
content: For ai-bookkeeper sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-3 stabilization: the seed reported a raw  in src/app/page.tsx flagged by @next/next/no-html-link-for-pages, blamed for holding cert-1 and cert-2 releases. Empirically FALSE in the committed tree — page.tsx:12 already uses , next/link imported, all page routes (/, /pricing, /faq, /dashboard) use ; only hash-anchor  (#how/#start) remain and those are not flagged; eslint . exits 0 (GREEN). Lesson (mirrors ADR 0004 'orphaned primitive' lesson): always verify a reported defect against the actual tree before scoping a fix — a seed's premise can be stale. Scoped the minimal scope-disciplined increment the seed still justified: ONE Playwright e2e guard test (e2e/home-nav.spec.ts), page.tsx untouched. Also caught a version mismatch: seed asked for PATCH 0.5.x but package.json is 0.4.0, so PATCH = 0.4.1 (0.5.x would be MINOR). Test tooling fact: repo has NO testing-library/jsdom — React component render tests are not feasible without adding deps; Playwright e2e (port 3100, auto webServer) is the only UI test surface; unit tests run via 'tsx --test src/**/*.test.ts'.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 21:15 UTC
cert-3 ADR-0006 home-nav guard: implemented as committed e2e test; 2 pre-existing e2e failures surfaced
content: ai-bookkeeper team-mode handoff (ADR 0006). The Architect's premise was confirmed: src/app/page.tsx:12 already uses  and eslint . exits 0 — the no-html-link-for-pages 'regression' was already fixed in tree, so the scoped work was the missing BEHAVIORAL guard only. Implemented exactly one file: e2e/home-nav.spec.ts (goto /pricing → click header 'Ledgerline' brand link → assert URL ends with /, no full reload via a window.__noReload flag, H1 visible). page.tsx untouched. Gate passed: lint 0, typecheck 0, new spec 1 passed. Committed b510ec9 on main, NOT pushed (review_each_pr human merge gate). KEY NEW FINDING: the FULL playwright suite has 2 PRE-EXISTING failures unrelated to this change — e2e/dashboard.spec.ts:12 (dashboard shell text 'Books current'/'AI Bookkeeper') and e2e/wealth-score.spec.ts:49 (playbook-upside pill regex). 104 passed / 2 failed. These are tracked unmodified specs failing on current HEAD and will block any full-suite-green release gate; they need independent tickets — mirrors the recurring 'pre-existing failure gates clean feature work' pattern (page.tsx lint blocked ADR-0004). Test surface facts: e2e baseURL auto-boots dev server on PORT 3210 (NOT 3100 as ADR text claimed); footer 'Ledgerline' is a plain  so getByRole('link',{name:/Ledgerline/}) resolves uniquely to the header brand link.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 21:20 UTC
ADR-0006 home-nav e2e guard reviewed APPROVE (b510ec9) — test-only change, gate re-run clean; 2 pre-existing e2e failures escalated as recurring pattern
content: Reviewed commit b510ec9 (ADR-0006: e2e/home-nav.spec.ts) on ai-bookkeeper. Verdict APPROVE, no security/correctness/critical blockers. This is a TEST-ONLY change (45 lines, single file, page.tsx untouched — confirmed via git show). Reviewer gate re-run independently: tsc --noEmit exit 0, eslint on the new file exit 0. Build-integrity PASS: the spec imports only @playwright/test (tracked devDep), no barrel re-exports → no untracked-sibling trap (contrast ADR-0002 b29eeaa). Correctness verified against source: pricing header brand  accessible name = img alt 'Ledgerline brand mark' + text 'Ledgerline' (matches /Ledgerline/); nav Home does NOT match; footer 'Ledgerline' is a  not a link → exactly one matching link, .first() is defensive; home page.tsx:84 has a single . The window.__noReload flag technique (set via page.evaluate after goto, asserted survived after click) is a valid SPA-vs-full-reload discriminator. Two LOW non-blocking notes: (1) line 34 toHaveURL(/\/$/) is a loose anchor matching any URL ending in '/' — prefer toHaveURL('/') which Playwright resolves against baseURL to the exact root; (2) line 30 locator couples to the brand wordmark — a data-testid would be rename-proof (optional, current getByRole approach follows repo convention). ESCALATED (not a finding against this change): full Playwright suite has 2 PRE-EXISTING failures in unmodified tracked files — dashboard.spec.ts:12, wealth-score.spec.ts:49 — failing on HEAD independent of b510ec9; a new test file cannot cause them. They block any full-suite-green release gate and need independent tickets. This is now the 3rd consecutive ledger work order where a pre-existing failure gates otherwise-clean work (page.tsx lint blocked ADR-0004) — confirmed RECURRING PATTERN, escalate as pattern not one-off. Reviewer value-add held: re-run the gate + integrity check, do not re-implement.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 21:24 UTC
ai-bookkeeper @ b510ec9: postcss CVE STILL OPEN (4th scan, no overrides); ingest.ts validation boundary added; layout.tsx dangerouslySetInnerHTML benign
content:
Re-scan at HEAD b510ec9 (after 12bc223). Major new code: customers (identity-resolution/normalize/resolve), tax-credits, benchmarks features, and ledger/lib/ingest.ts. SECURITY STATUS:

1) postcss XSS GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (<8.5.10) STILL OPEN — 4th consecutive scan, overrides block STILL not added to package.json. Top-level postcss is 8.5.15 (devDep ^8.5.15) but next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31 at package-lock.json:3485, unreachable by top-level bump. npm audit = 2 moderate (postcss + next.via=['postcss'], same single root cause). Fix = one-line overrides {"postcss":">=8.5.10"}; npm's next@9.3.3 suggestion is a wrong breaking downgrade. Ranked High per known-CVE policy, real reachability low (build-time CSS stringify). Next re-scan: grep package.json for overrides block before re-reporting.

2) @google/generative-ai ^0.24.1 (package.json:13) STILL unused in src/ — Low, still open. Remove until server integration; no NEXT_PUBLIC_ on secret keys when added.

3) NEW but BENIGN: src/app/layout.tsx:88 dangerouslySetInnerHTML for themeInitScript — __html is a STATIC constant (no interpolation, no user input), standard Next.js anti-FOUC pattern. NOT a finding/not exploitable; logged Low-informational. Guidance: stays safe only while the string remains a literal.

4) NEW ledger/lib/ingest.ts is security-POSITIVE: single pure fail-loud ingest boundary that validates amount via parseAmount before projecting RawTransaction->Transaction; designed so future Twilio/Plaid adapters can't trust upstream amounts. No injection surface.

Rest CLEAN: 0 secrets (only .env.example tracked; .env/.gcp/*.pem gitignored), 0 eval/exec/process.env in src, no app/api routes, localStorage theme-only, no fetch/network, no PII logging. COVERAGE GAP: Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable again — could not consult/write team knowledge graph (3rd run with this gap).

## Security Scan Report — 2026-05-28

**Codebase:** `ai-bookkeeper` @ `b510ec9` (frontend-only Next.js 16 / React 19, mock data) · **Coverage:** dependency audit ✓ · secret detection ✓ · risky-pattern ✓ · **Gap:** Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable this run.

**Counts — Critical: 0 · High: 1 · Medium: 0 · Low: 2**

```chart
Critical: 0
High: 1
Medium: 0
Low: 2
```

### 🟠 High (1)
- **postcss <8.5.10 — XSS via unescaped `` in CSS stringify output** (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) · `package-lock.json:3485` (nested `node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss@8.4.31`). **STILL OPEN — 4th scan.** Top-level postcss is 8.5.15, but `next@16.2.6` hard-pins postcss 8.4.31 transitively; a top-level devDep bump cannot reach it. `npm audit` = 2 moderate (postcss + next, single root cause). **Fix:** add to `package.json` → `"overrides": { "postcss": ">=8.5.10" }`, then `npm install`. Do **not** take npm's suggested `next@9.3.3` — it is a breaking major downgrade. Ranked High per known-CVE policy; real reachability low (build-time CSS only).

### 🟡 Low (2)
- **Unused production dependency `@google/generative-ai@^0.24.1`** · `package.json:13`. **STILL OPEN.** Declared but imported nowhere in `src/` — needless supply-chain surface. **Fix:** remove until server integration lands; when added, keep `GEMINI_API_KEY`/`GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` server-side (no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix).
- **`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` for theme-init script** · `src/app/layout.tsx:88`. **NEW — reviewed, not exploitable.** `__html` is a static constant (`themeInitScript`), no interpolation or user input — the standard Next.js anti-FOUC pattern. **Guidance:** keep this string a literal; never interpolate runtime/user values into it.

### ✅ Clean
0 hardcoded secrets (only `.env.example` tracked; `.env`/`.gcp`/`*.pem` gitignored). 0 injection/eval/`process.env` reads in `src/`. No `app/api` routes. New `ingest.ts` is a pure fail-loud validation boundary (security-positive). localStorage use is theme-only (non-sensitive).
